claim: Enabling auto-test flags in AI coding assistants (running tests after every prompt) creates a tight feedback loop that automatically validates code, catching hallucinations and bugs immediately
actionable: Configure your AI coding assistant (Aider, Cursor) with auto-test enabled; this transforms code generation from a one-shot write into an iterative validate-and-refine cycle.
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion

claim: A conventions file (markdown with coding rules and style guidelines) guides AI coding assistants to write consistent, high-quality code aligned with your project standards without explicit per-prompt instruction
actionable: Create a conventions.md file at your project root documenting coding patterns, type conventions, naming schemes, and architectural decisions; reference it in your AI assistant config.
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion

claim: The pattern 'action + file + description' (e.g., 'update index.ts to add CLI commands') gives AI coding assistants atomic, scoped contexts that improve accuracy over free-form requests
actionable: When prompting AI coding assistants, use structured prompts: [action] [file(s)] [specific change description] rather than conversational requests.
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion

claim: AI coding assistants can propagate changes across multiple files in a single prompt when you specify them explicitly (e.g., 'update notion.ts, test.ts, and index.ts to add delete functionality')
actionable: Batch related changes across files in a single prompt to the assistant rather than making incremental single-file changes; this maintains consistency and reduces iteration.
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion

claim: Using a dot configuration file (e.g., .aider.yaml) lets you globally configure AI assistant behavior per codebase: prompt caching, auto-test flags, read-only files, and context files
actionable: Create a .aider.yaml (or equivalent) config file in your project root to set auto-test, enable prompt caching, and pin conventions/readme files in context.
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion

claim: Claude 3.5 Sonnet is the best-in-class model for AI-assisted coding at the time of this video; it significantly outperforms other models in code generation accuracy
actionable: Use Claude 3.5 Sonnet as your default model for AI coding assistants; consider downgrading to cheaper/faster models only for simple refactors or documentation.
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion

claim: Prompting with 'mirror' keyword triggers the AI coding assistant to replicate patterns already established in your codebase (method signatures, test structure) to new files automatically
actionable: Use 'mirror' keyword in prompts to AI coding assistants when you want it to replicate patterns from existing code: 'create a delete method mirroring the add method pattern.'
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion

claim: AI coding with tight test feedback loops increases engineering velocity so dramatically that code quality can improve even with minor hallucinations, because the assistant self-corrects via test failures
actionable: Accept that AI coding tools will hallucinate or make mistakes; rely on auto-test feedback and review cycles to catch and fix them, prioritizing speed over theoretical perfection.
source_title: SECRET SAUCE of AI Coding? AI Devlog with Aider, Cursor, Bun and Notion
